[2024-06-29 20:40] silentslayers: I think it's pretty fair to consider it like you absorb on the feral warden, and then convert it from a wall into an attacker. If the game never goes into both players breaching each other, it's essentially the same as absorbing 2. I also prefer to think on these terms for Protoplasm, where I get 3 absorb and then convert my 4 HP into 4 gauss charges. So if your goal is to get an attacker (feral) or your goal is to get tempo (protoplasm), then they're both very efficient since you're getting some HP tempo while getting attackers/gauss charges.
[2024-06-29 20:41] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): Hmm, idk, to me absorb implies I have defense tempo next turn
[2024-06-29 20:42] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): But a converted Wall (Splitter defense -> swing, Feral defense -> swing, etc.) do not provide that
[2024-06-29 20:42] silentslayers: To me absorb implies I have cheated HP compared to letting all the swords deal all the damage by letting something still live and give value.
[2024-06-29 20:42] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): Splitter defense is absorb though
[2024-06-29 20:42] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): But if it is swung there is no defense tempo
[2024-06-29 20:43] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): I guess absorb gives the option of defense tempo
[2024-06-29 20:43] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): On Splitter for example
[2024-06-29 20:43] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): On Wall, it's forced, since it's just there
[2024-06-29 20:43] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): But on something like Feral, there is no defense tempo option, once it has lost its 2/3 HP
[2024-06-29 20:44] iminabearsuit: I would say hellhound engineer is absorb. So Im a terminology rebel here.
[2024-06-29 20:44] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): Well, it can still defend for 1 but
[2024-06-29 20:44] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): To me all those things are soak
[2024-06-29 20:44] iminabearsuit: Hellhound upgrades your wall next turn from 3 to 4
[2024-06-29 20:44] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): Absorb only works if it's not exploited though
[2024-06-29 20:44] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): Hellhound gives 1 granular soak
[2024-06-29 20:45] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): So I wouldn't consider it to upg my wall for 1 turn cos that would imply that it can get exploited
[2024-06-29 20:46] iminabearsuit: In my head. Its just, if I can buy hellhound + wall soak every turn. The outcome is the  same as the player buying fast tarsier, and absorbing on a doomed wall
[2024-06-29 20:46] silentslayers: It's fine for everyone to have their own definition. :D
[2024-06-29 20:48] iminabearsuit: It is just a mental shortcut, that saying a sentinel, or hellhound farm. Equates to having a higher absorber, for evaluating the board.
[2024-06-29 20:48] silentslayers: it gives soak per turn, very similar to what absorb does
[2024-06-29 20:48] iminabearsuit: But Im definietly bottom right in the "aborb sandwich chart"
[2024-06-29 20:48] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): Yep defo

And I ain't tryna start a heated debate or nothin' ^-^

However, I think it is useful to have convos like this and see how different ppl think, and discuss terminology

I think it's good to have agreed upon terms for easier comms, especially when it comes to relaying info / teaching newer peepz
[2024-06-29 20:50] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): But regarding the mental shortcut thing, I agree, and I think that's a good way to think
[2024-06-29 20:51] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): (But it can potentially be confusing to newer players, if that was not clarified or taught to them before)
[2024-06-29 20:51] silentslayers: If it was agreed upon earlier to be more careful with definitions I’d agree it’s good to all use same stuff. At this point I don’t really care. Most people know when I say “absorb on X” I mean to ctrl click it as your absorber, whether it’s fragile or not.
[2024-06-29 20:53] lyra1712 (Lyra): this, i even say abosrb on engi if i mean ctrl click engi to defend
[2024-06-29 20:53] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): Yea I generally don't care either, and I'd understand what someone means if they said "absorb on X"

But in the context of a newer player asking questions, I'd care more and go out of my way to preemptively clarify just in case

[2024-06-29 20:54] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): Though that is how my mind operates, not necessarily "the best approach"
[2024-06-29 20:54] zezetel: I also think of feral as absorb even if it technically maybe isn't

[2024-11-21 00:46] .holyfire (Holy-Fire): That was an interesting set.
Zemora + Synthesizer + Cluster + Xaetron.
Probably neither I nor Master Bot played close to optimal, but still, getting 5 Cluster bolts on the turn leading to Zemora activation was fun.
8oFXt-@Tth4
[2024-11-21 00:59] silentslayers: Fun set indeed. I win a lot of these cluster zem sets. A big thing people get wrong is to get too little conduits and to wait too long to cluster. It's ok to cluster with no permanent damage behind it before zemora comes online. It just needs to deflate your opponent to be worth it. If you wait too long and they do it to you, then you end up force fielding vs the clusters and you can't cluster back immediately since it makes your defense harder (defending 1 more dmg per cluster you make). Wasn't relevant to do it in this game though.

I liked the way you played it. Zem timing looked good, blast into synth spam was good I think. Maybe turn 7 you're supposed to Xae + Conduit? Feels bad not synthing though, but 1 turn earlier on the Xae is pretty sweet, it makes 4 extra HP compared to DDC + Synth which is what you did, so it's 4 HP instead of DD + Synth in terms of the 1 turn of growth. So 2 gold + 2 green for 4 hp. Actually both lines look good.

Unfortunately a bit of a non-game cause master bot botched the opening. 😄

[2024-11-28 16:06] silentslayers: Oh sorry. So about the decision to have 2 gold for the engineer or to buy Drone vs Tarsier vs Whatever:

Buying a drone costs 2 gold since it mines immediately, so it is basically instead of an engineer. If the game is high tempo, any spreadsheet math will tell you the engineer is incredible and the drone is whatever. But sometimes games force you to antima open, but then slow down and get a big absorber, and if it's one of those sets, some drones can be good. Especially if the opponent has a relatively easy defense so an extra engineer or two doesn't even change their defense.

On the topic of drones, you can actually calculate your exact starting gold after your Shift+E turn and decide based on that whether you want any drones. Like if I have 13 gold after Shift+E and I want 14 gold so I can TT+DD after, that drone becomes a bit better.

A tarsier is usually almost always worth it because it's only 1 less pressure than an engineer and you get a permanent attacker instead of 2 HP and 1 ATK, which is typically a good deal (especially because the 2 HP is pretty worthless since you're over-defended). If I'm 1 green short of a cluster sometimes I grab a conduit. The only other tech building I'd buy would be if I could buy a tech building to buy a hound or a caut I think

The actual Shift+E turn itself, sometimes I don't Shift+E but it's pretty rare. Exceptions are quick units like immolites/smorcus/militia/vore/nivo, obvious things like engineer producing units (cauts/hellhounds/tesla), tarsiers if it's an ampo game sometimes.

[2024-11-29 00:10] .holyfire (Holy-Fire): 1. There are more efficient ways for non-prompt defense than 2 gold each. e.g. - I buy 4 engis for 8 gold, they build an infusion grid for the equivalent of 6.7 gold and counteract it.

2. The gold I spend is half a turn earlier than them. Adjusting for that, the 8 gold I spend is actually equivalent to about 9.4 gold they would spend after. And 9.4 is significantly higher than 6.7.
[2024-11-29 00:12] .holyfire (Holy-Fire): Of course, the turn after buying antima isn't much of a question - 2 gold for an attack next turn is a good deal, even without the extra defense you get.
[2024-11-29 00:12] lezaleas: So you are getting 4 engis for 1.3g vs infusion?
[2024-11-29 00:12] lezaleas: 4 engis for 1.3g is awesome
[2024-11-29 00:13] lezaleas: Antima isn't supposed to make up for its cost in offensive value alone
[2024-11-29 00:13] lezaleas: Most of its value is that you trade engis into their defense evenly and you are left with the engis
[2024-11-29 00:14] lezaleas: Also your argument 2 doesnt really hold
[2024-11-29 00:14] lezaleas: Any attack unit you get takes half a turn of set up
[2024-11-29 00:15] lezaleas: The only units that have value right now are promp blockers but they have reduced marginal value
[2024-11-29 00:15] lezaleas: Oh yo are asking for the turn you build antima
[2024-11-29 00:15] lezaleas: Yeah that one is closer
[2024-11-29 00:16] .holyfire (Holy-Fire): Yes. Like I said, for the turn after there isn't much of a question.
[2024-11-29 00:16] lezaleas: A trick you can do is multiply your value for 3/4 for each turn of delay on efficiency since its the rate at which drones grows econ
[2024-11-29 00:17] lezaleas: If you do that they are not worth it but they are not that bad either
[2024-11-29 00:18] .holyfire (Holy-Fire): Drones are only efficient in the early game. The so-called "inflation" rate increases as the game progresses. With Tarsiers as the main attackers, the ratio is more like 5/7.
[2024-11-29 00:19] lezaleas: Yeah but usually you are not past blocking during antima turns
[2024-11-29 00:19] lezaleas: So your rate is 4/3 at that turn
[2024-11-29 00:19] lezaleas: Wait how do you get 5/7 from a tarsier?
[2024-11-29 00:20] .holyfire (Holy-Fire): There's a calculation, there's a famous page that shows it but I forget how it's called
[2024-11-29 00:21] lezaleas: I'm not sure it's the same thing I'm referring to
[2024-11-29 00:21] .holyfire (Holy-Fire): I think it's equivalent
[2024-11-29 00:23] silentslayers: What is "they" in this sentence?
[2024-11-29 00:23] silentslayers: the engineers or what?
[2024-11-29 00:23] lezaleas: Antima build turn engis
[2024-11-29 00:23] lezaleas: They are worth a little bit more than 2g on turn 2 antima
[2024-11-29 00:23] .holyfire (Holy-Fire): The calculation shows that when the main unit you buy is tarsier, you have about r=1.4. That is equivalent to saying that getting a thing next turn is worth 5/7 as much as getting it this turn.
[2024-11-29 00:24] lezaleas: So they are worth a bit more than 1.5 on antima turn 1
[2024-11-29 00:24] lezaleas: Yeah but i dont understand where the 1.4 comes from
[2024-11-29 00:24] lezaleas: Is it being compared to walls?
[2024-11-29 00:25] .holyfire (Holy-Fire): I think so, let me reproduce the calculation
[2024-11-29 00:25] lezaleas: You can't use tarsiers to build tarsiers i mean
[2024-11-29 00:25] lezaleas: But you can figure out how many tarsiers you could build with your opponents wall gold
[2024-11-29 00:25] lezaleas: Wall gold being your attack
[2024-11-29 00:26] silentslayers: You can simulate that though, we call that recursive inflation
[2024-11-29 00:26] .holyfire (Holy-Fire): It's assumed that 1 point of prompt defense has a certain gold cost, called barrier value
[2024-11-29 00:26] silentslayers: What are you assuming here? Just the damage you get? Or when do you get the defensive value?
[2024-11-29 00:26] .holyfire (Holy-Fire): I think it's usually taken to be BV = 2.2 or 2.3
[2024-11-29 00:26] lezaleas: Yeah you are transforming attack into gold using barrier value
[2024-11-29 00:26] .holyfire (Holy-Fire): And you calculate how many of those a tarsier gives you effectively
[2024-11-29 00:27] lezaleas: 2.22 soemthing is the barrier value of a wall
[2024-11-29 00:27] .holyfire (Holy-Fire): Building a tarsier requires that your opponent builds a barrier in 1.5 turns, and then every turn
[2024-11-29 00:27] .holyfire (Holy-Fire): So you sum the geometric series r ^ -1.5 + r^ -2.5 + r^-3.5 etc.
[2024-11-29 00:27] lezaleas: So for a turn 2 engi
[2024-11-29 00:28] lezaleas: We get 2.2g from barrier value
[2024-11-29 00:28] lezaleas: And then some defense tempo
[2024-11-29 00:28] .holyfire (Holy-Fire): That's 1/ ((r-1)sqrt(r))
[2024-11-29 00:28] .holyfire (Holy-Fire): Multiply that by a barrier value of 2.2
[2024-11-29 00:28] lezaleas: Wihich is 2.2 * 3/4^delay
[2024-11-29 00:28] .holyfire (Holy-Fire): And a Tarsier costs 5
[2024-11-29 00:29] lezaleas: So the engi is probably around 2.5g maybe even less
